context_pack.industry_context: The strawberry industry is focused on sustainable farming practices and maximizing yield. Recent trends include the use of vertical farming and hydroponics to grow strawberries in controlled environments, which can lead to higher productivity and less pesticide use.

terminology_glossary.Hydroponics: A method of growing plants without soil, using mineral nutrient solutions in an aqueous solvent.
terminology_glossary.Vertical Farming: The practice of growing crops in stacked layers, often incorporating controlled-environment agriculture.
terminology_glossary.Pesticide: Substances used for destroying insects or other organisms harmful to cultivated plants.

typical_workflows.traditional_process: Traditional strawberry farming involves selecting the right variety, preparing the soil, planting during the correct season, providing adequate water, and managing pests. Optimized methods might include hydroponic systems where strawberries are grown in nutrient solutions, allowing for year-round production and reduced labor.

key_points: Utilize vertical farming for higher productivity
Implement hydroponics to reduce pesticide use

description: Reducing labor costs with automated systems
